figures_detected.description: Crankshaft key position — shows crankshaft with key at top dead centre position
Lower timing chain installation — shows mating marks (yellow) on chain and sprockets, 18 links on each side, same number link callout, mating marks on crankshaft sprocket
Front cover with liquid gasket application — bead width 2.0–3.0 mm (0.079–0.118 in) indicated
Front cover rear face showing O-ring positions

steps.text: Confirm that No. 1 piston is set at T.D.C. on its compression stroke.
Install crankshaft sprocket and lower timing chain.
notes: Set timing chain by aligning its mating mark with the one on crankshaft sprocket.
Make sure sprocket's mating mark faces engine front.
The number of links between the alignment marks (silver) are the same for the left and right sides, so either side can be used during alignment with the sprocket.

steps.text: Fit the lower chain tensioner and the chain guide.
Apply liquid gasket to front cover.

notes: Check alignment of mating marks on chain and crankshaft sprocket.
Align oil drive spacer with oil pump.
Before installing front cover remove all traces of old liquid gasket.
Be careful not to damage oil seal when installing front cover.

steps.text: Install oil pan. Refer to "Installation" in "OIL PAN".

items.label: Liquid gasket bead width on front cover
items.condition: Applied to front cover mating surface

items.label: Timing chain links between alignment marks (left side)
items.condition: Between mating marks on lower timing chain

items.label: Timing chain links between alignment marks (right side)
items.condition: Between mating marks on lower timing chain
